errors: The ELM implementation has several discrepancies with the CPG requirements.
The ELM implementation has several mismatches with the CPG requirements.

The ELM implementation has a mismatch in the age threshold. The CPG requires Age >= 15 years AND Age <= 65 years, while the ELM implementation uses Age >= 30 years. This is a significant difference in the inclusion criteria and will lead to incorrect screening recommendations.  The time interval is correct (1 year), but the age threshold is not.

The ELM implementation has a mismatch in the age threshold. The CPG states “Age >= 18 years”, while the ELM implementation specifies “Age >= 25 years”. This violates the inclusion criteria. Additionally, the “Has Recent Alcohol Screening” logic is overly restrictive. It checks for *any* observation with the specified value set within the past year, regardless of the result. The CPG states “No screening needed if… a result present”, implying a positive screening result should exclude the need for further screening.
